purpose: Separate replay and editor-authorization checks from mechanistic evidence and exploration diagnostics.

modes.play: An instrumented policy chooses a Pixie action; its decision receipt must link to the exact executed action and replayed episode.
modes.editor: An instrumented policy proposes one typed profile edit; a structural firewall authorizes a copied-profile mutation before paired execution and replay.

hard_checks.contract: Observation, activation, logits, selected output, intervention, and record hashes re-derive exactly.
A play decision's selected action equals every executed Pixie action receipt.
The proposal targets the observed critter, one allowlisted scalar, a bounded delta, and an immutable parent snapshot.
Exactly one copied profile scalar changes from the recorded old value to the recorded new value.
The executed play or edited life-form episode replays to the same deterministic projection.
No probe, attribution, activation, representation, complexity, delight, or response-diversity metric appears in editor authorization gates.

mechanistic_evidence.role: Measure held-out linear decodability; not sufficient for causal interpretation.
Measure whether a registered hidden unit causally contributes to the chosen logit.
Measure whether transplanting identity-unit activations changes the selected output.

authorization_rule: Only hard checks may authorize or promote an edit. Mechanistic evidence and diagnostics may trigger review, ablation experiments, or data collection but cannot grant write authority.
claim_boundary: The canary establishes harness wiring against known internal structure. It does not establish that a learned agent's features have the same semantics or that a decoded feature is causally complete.
